title: Substrate bootstrap hardcodes .project/ — config-driven-from-line-1 claim unrealized at bootstrap edge
description:
Step 2 substrate SDK port (commit d4ec3ce) commit message claims 'config-driven vocabulary registries from line 1'. ConfigBlock declares a `root` field; getProjectContext / loadConfig / lens-view / roadmap-plan all consume config.root for substrate operations. But the bootstrap edge — WHERE pi-context looks for config.json before it can read config.root — is hardcoded to `<cwd>/.project/config.json` via PROJECT_DIR + SCHEMAS_DIR constants in packages/pi-context/src/project-dir.ts. Every block-api primitive composes blockFilePath(cwd, blockName) = path.join(cwd, '.project', `${blockName}.json`); blockSchemaPath similarly. config.root is read AFTER bootstrap; it cannot relocate the bootstrap dir itself. Result: user cannot choose the substrate dir name. The 'config is the source of truth' substrate principle is structurally undelivered — config.root only relocates substrate state INSIDE the hardcoded `.project/` location, not the location itself.

Secondary: subcommand surface is `/project init`, `/project install`, `/project view`, etc. — package was renamed pi-project → pi-context at Step 1 but command surface kept the old `/project` namespace. The command name implies `.project/` is the dir; the package implies `/context`. Both contradict config.root being the source of truth.

LLM rationalization in HANDOFF.md called this a 'Bootstrap exemption: config.json + relations.json continue at <cwd>/.project/' — that framing is LLM hedging, not a design decision. The exemption was never authored by user direction; it post-hoc justifies an incomplete fix as if it were deliberate.

DEC-0015 (enacted 2026-05-10) anchors the underlying principle ('config drives substrate location; no hardcoded substrate-dir paths anywhere') as permanent substrate canon. FGAP-026 is the current-state implementation-gap record for DEC-0015 — closes (status:closed) when the resolver + cascades + bootstrap pointer schema + /context init + migration ceremony land. The DEC-0015 principle remains permanent after FGAP-026 closes; future tool/primitive additions are checked against DEC-0015 even with FGAP-026 in closed state. Reopening DEC-0015 violations gets filed as new FGAPs (regression record), not by reopening FGAP-026.

evidence.reference: PROJECT_DIR = '.project' + SCHEMAS_DIR = 'schemas' constants. Single point where the substrate dir name is hardcoded.
blockFilePath(cwd, blockName) and blockSchemaPath(cwd, blockName) compose against PROJECT_DIR — every block-api primitive (16 of them post-Step-6.3) inherits the hardcode via these helpers.
PROJECT_SUBCOMMANDS register under '/project' namespace (init, install, view, lens-curate, roadmap-list/view/validate). Package renamed to pi-context at Step 1 but command surface kept '/project'.
ConfigBlock.root field declared but loadConfig itself reads from path.join(cwd, PROJECT_DIR, 'config.json') — root field can describe the substrate dir but cannot relocate WHERE config is found.
HANDOFF text 'Bootstrap exemption: config.json + relations.json continue at <cwd>/.project/' frames the hardcode as design when it is LLM rationalization of unaddressed work.

impact: Substrate-arc thesis structurally undelivered at the bootstrap edge. User cannot pick a substrate dir name; config.root indirection is cosmetic (relocates blocks within a hardcoded outer dir). Onboarding UX for new users (or LLMs) requires accepting `.project/` regardless of project conventions. Other consumers (pi-jit-agents, pi-workflows, pi-behavior-monitors) inherit the hardcode via block-api imports. Multi-substrate scenarios (a repo wanting two parallel substrates) impossible. The 'config-driven from line 1' commit-message claim creates a credibility gap with the actual code.
proposed_resolution: Replace PROJECT_DIR constant with per-cwd `resolveContextDir(cwd): string` reading a JSON-typed bootstrap pointer at `<cwd>/.pi-context.json` (carries the user-chosen substrate dir name; schema authored under packages/pi-context/schemas/bootstrap.schema.json with $id pi-context://schemas/bootstrap + version 1.0.0 per FGAP-006 discipline). Update blockFilePath / blockSchemaPath in block-api.ts to compose against the resolver. Update loadConfig in project-context.ts to find config.json at `<cwd>/<resolveContextDir(cwd)>/config.json`. Cascade through pi-jit-agents / pi-workflows / pi-behavior-monitors path-construction sites. Rename `/project` subcommand surface to `/context`. Rename `project-*` pi tool names to `context-*` (full rename; no aliases — rebuild branch unpublished, no downstream consumer). New `/context init` subcommand: prompt-required for the dir name (defaulting reintroduces the hardcode-dressed-as-default pattern that FGAP-026 itself calls out); creates `<dir-name>/config.json` with root: '<dir-name>' AND writes `<cwd>/.pi-context.json` containing the dir name. Migration ceremony for existing `.project/`-using repos: `pi context migrate` renames `.project/` → `.context/` (or to user-chosen name) AND writes `<cwd>/.pi-context.json` containing the new name. The four 'open design questions' the original proposed_resolution surfaced (tool-name rename scope, bootstrap pointer format, migration default, init UX when no arg) were LLM hedging — none represent real design space. Each has a single mandate-compliant answer per the architectural premise. Corrected proposed_resolution embeds the answers directly.
